commit.message:
Add email + Telegram push notifications on every event

- Email via Resend API (free 100/day, set RESEND_API_KEY + NOTIFY_EMAIL)
- Telegram via Bot API (set TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN + TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID)
- Both fire-and-forget on every notify() call
- Styled HTML email with dark theme matching brand
- Markdown formatting for Telegram messages
- Falls back silently if env vars not set

feat(hr-interview): 2-min grace period when timer expires mid-answer

When the 15-min timer hits 0 while the candidate is recording or has
unsent transcript text, instead of immediately ending:
- Stop the microphone, show an amber banner with a 2-min countdown
- Candidate can review, re-record, or send their answer normally
- On send during grace, the answer is submitted then the interview ends
  (no new question is asked even if the API returns one)
- If grace period expires without submitting, auto-submit pending text
  then end; if there is no text, end immediately

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix flood water rendering: use idle event instead of setTimeout

The setTimeout(300) fires while fitBounds animation is in progress,
causing isStyleLoaded() to return false and skipping flood rendering.
Now uses map.once('idle') which fires after all tiles are loaded.
Also removes isStyleLoaded() guard from moveend handler since
queryRenderedFeatures is already wrapped in try-catch.

commit.message:
feat: add audio, website, and image OCR converters

Add three new conversion modes to complement the existing PDF converter:
- Audio → Markdown: whisper.cpp local engine + OpenAI cloud, live mic recording, speaker detection
- Website → Markdown: reader-mode extraction with SSRF protection, DOM walking, image download
- Image OCR → Markdown: tesseract CLI (no build-time C deps) + OpenAI Vision cloud fallback

Also includes PDF pipeline improvements (table detection, gap-based merging,
text-edge column detection), comprehensive security hardening (HTTP timeouts,
size limits, URL validation), and updated documentation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add animated Argonauts to hero section — v1.3.2

- 6 blob characters (yellow/blue/red/green/purple/gray) built with Three.js
- Walk in from right on page load, staggered entry per character
- Idle: breathing (body scale), gentle sway, eye blink (independent timing)
- Hover: arm raises (random left/right, wave oscillation), spring physics
- Each character has unique personality (speed, phase, timing)
- Lazy-loaded via React.Suspense — zero impact on initial page load
- Hidden on mobile (md:block), orthographic camera for flat cartoon look
- MeshToonMaterial + gradient map for cel-shaded aesthetic

fix: solucionar bug navegación footer - scroll a 0 después de matar GSAP + RAF

Tres cambios clave para el bug de página en blanco al primer click en footer:

1. Footer.jsx: usa kill(true) para revertir animaciones, resetea más
   propiedades CSS (overflowY, paddingRight, position, top), fuerza
   scroll a 0 DESPUÉS de matar GSAP, y navega dentro de requestAnimationFrame
   para dar tiempo a los callbacks RAF de GSAP a completarse.

2. App.jsx useLayoutEffect: mata GSAP PRIMERO y luego hace scrollTo(0,0)
   (el orden anterior era incorrecto - scrollTo corría antes del kill,
   permitiendo a GSAP sobreescribir el reset de scroll).

commit.message:
fix: Use correct snake_case field names when updating promo

Critical bug fix:
- updatePromo() expects snake_case field names (database schema)
- Was passing camelCase field names from form data
- This caused edited values to not actually update in database

Fixed fields:
- promoName → promo_name
- retailerName → retailer_name
- promoStartDate → promo_start_date
- promoEndDate → promo_end_date
- storeSalePercentage → store_sale_percentage
- producerPaybackPercentage → producer_payback_percentage
- demoHoursEntries → demo_hours_entries

This fixes the issue where editing a promo and changing the payback % (or any other field) didn't actually update the calculations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix map race condition: add mapReady state to both map components

Ensures sensor dots and flood water render after Mapbox finishes loading,
preventing the data effect from exiting early when isStyleLoaded() is false.

fix: add --min-instances=1 to prod Cloud Run deploy

Prevents 429 "Rate exceeded" errors caused by cold starts
when instances scale to zero between requests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Full ERD PNG export by inlining SVG into DOM

SVGs with <foreignObject> (used by Mermaid for text labels) always
taint the canvas when rendered via drawImage(), making toBlob() fail.
Fix by fetching the SVG text, inlining it into a hidden DOM container,
and using html2canvas on the inline SVG instead of drawing an Image.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Prevent duplicate draft promos and fix edit functionality

Bug fixes:
1. Multiple drafts issue:
   - Previously created NEW draft every time "Analyze Promo" was clicked
   - Now checks if editing existing promo or re-analyzing current promo
   - Updates existing promo instead of creating duplicates
   - Logic: editPromoId → use that, analysisResult → update existing, else → create new

2. Edit button not loading promo:
   - Added setSubmittedFormData when loading promo for editing
   - Form now fully populates with all saved data
   - Edit flow now works correctly from Promo Tracker

Workflow now:
- First analyze: Creates draft
- Re-analyze same promo: Updates draft (no duplicate)
- Edit from tracker: Loads promo, updates on re-analyze
- Approve/Decline: Updates status, no new drafts created

Add cone of influence highlighting on node click

Both interactions integrate with search (mutual clearing).

commit.message:
Fix trending artists: use substring genre matching instead of exact key lookup

Spotify returns specific subgenres like 'atl hip hop', 'melodic rap', 'bedroom pop'
that never matched the exact-key GENRE_TO_CATEGORY object. Replace with a
getGenreCategory() function using includes() checks so any variant maps correctly.

Break up god files, add item system and persistence tests

Split game.d.ts (2,358 lines) into 8 domain-specific type files:
content, combat, items, world-nodes, run, profile, api, and ui.

Extract account-meta markup builders from ui-common.ts (1,002 -> 221 lines)
into ui-account-meta.ts, preserving the ROUGE_UI_COMMON public contract.

Add item-system.test.ts (24 tests) covering catalog creation, lookups,
inventory operations, sockets, combat bonuses, and loadout summary.

Add persistence.test.ts (17 tests) covering snapshot/profile round-trips,
storage operations, profile meta, run history, and full lifecycle.

Fix missing UI state fields in app-engine createAppState initialization.

commit.message:
Smart elevation-based flood water on streets, dense sensor placement

- Remove ALL circle-based flood layers (no more blue radius)
- Add smart water flow simulation using IDW elevation interpolation
- Water fills lowest streets first, rises with flood severity
- Road geometry from Mapbox vector tiles for realistic "water on streets"
- Redesign seed: 20 sensors packed in one Aventura neighborhood
- Sensors placed at actual street intersections with elevation gradient
- Biscayne Corridor (low, flood-prone) → West Aventura (high, dry)
- Flood events biased toward low-elevation sensors for realism

repository.description: Flood Finder Smart City

commit.message:
Fix command routing: remove F.chat.type filter, add internal guards

Magic filter F.chat.type == "private" with Command() was silently blocking
handlers in aiogram 3.4.1. Replaced with inline check:
  if message.chat.type != "private" or not is_admin/moderator: return

This guarantees commands work in private chat regardless of aiogram filter quirks.
Also removes F.chat.type from Command decorators, keeping logic in handler body.

commit.message:
fix: auto-resolve cross-company invoice access for print/PDF export

When a user views or prints an invoice that belongs to a different
company than their active session, the backend now transparently
checks if the user has sales:read permission in that company and
serves the invoice from there. This eliminates the raw JSON error
shown in print/PDF dialogs and deep-links.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Footer usa handleNavigate con limpieza GSAP sincrónica + logo DayByDay

La causa real del bug: <Link> de React Router navega primero y luego
el useLayoutEffect limpia GSAP — demasiado tarde.

Solución: reemplazar todos los <Link> del footer por botones con
handleNavigate() que limpia ScrollTrigger y overflow ANTES de llamar
a navigate(path), igual que handleNavScroll del NavBar.

Cambios:
- Importar gsap, ScrollTrigger y useNavigate en Footer
- handleNavigate(): kills GSAP + reset body overflow → navigate(path)
- Reemplazar todos los <Link> con <button onClick={handleNavigate}>
- Añadir logo Day·by·Day en la parte superior del footer
  con link a "/" usando el mismo estilo del blog (by en rojo #de0015)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix redundant edge labels rendering behind lines and not being clickable

Redundant connection paths (outer thick stroke + white inner gap) were
capturing pointer events and visually covering edge labels. Added
pointerEvents: 'none' to both SVG paths and raised label z-index to 1000.
